artPrompt: A cheerful woman in a quilted vest strides up a dewy front walk at sunrise, fist raised mid-knock at a picket-fence gate, a clipboard with a blank page tucked under one arm and a steaming travel mug clutched in the other hand. Long golden shadows stretch across tidy lawns dotted with blank placards. Dew sparkles, birds scatter from a hedge, her determined grin lit by warm dawn light, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Early-Bird Canvasser
text:
Haste. (She started knocking before the coffee finished brewing.)
When Early-Bird Canvasser dies, you gain 1 life.
flavorText: 7 a.m. on a Saturday. Somewhere, a mayor's approval rating shudders in its sleep.
